heaveHo2ReleaseDatePricePlatforms.title: Heave Ho 2 Release Date, Price and Platform Comparison
heaveHo2ReleaseDatePricePlatforms.intro: Heave Ho 2 launched on July 16, 2026 for Windows PC, Nintendo Switch, and Nintendo Switch 2. All three versions support local and online multiplayer for up to four players, while the native Nintendo Switch 2 edition adds local and online GameShare.

platforms.name: Windows PC
platforms.demo: Free Steam demo with local and online co-op for 2-4 players

platforms.name: Nintendo Switch
platforms.players: Up to 4 players on one system or online

platforms.name: Nintendo Switch 2
platforms.players: Up to 4 players on one system or online
highlights: One owner shares with up to three players

heaveHo2ReleaseDatePricePlatforms.languageNote: All versions support English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Japanese, Korean, Brazilian Portuguese, Russian, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese.

heaveHo2BeginnerGuide.title: Heave Ho 2 Beginner Guide
heaveHo2BeginnerGuide.intro: Most campaign stages ask a team of 2-4 players to travel from the start to the finish by grabbing platforms, objects, and each other. Learn the basic loop of gripping, chaining, swinging, and recovering before tackling the eight themed worlds.

steps.title: Learn Your Two Hands
steps.instruction: Your character is a head with two independently controlled gripping hands. Practice holding a safe surface with one hand while reaching for the next grip with the other.
steps.teamTip: Call out left hand or right hand before releasing when another player depends on your grip.

steps.title: Create a Reliable Anchor
steps.instruction: Before a long crossing, place one player on a secure platform and keep at least one of that player's hands attached to the environment.
steps.teamTip: The anchored player should move as little as possible while teammates reposition.

steps.title: Build a Human Chain
steps.instruction: Grab a teammate's hand, head, or body and extend the group outward from the anchor. Longer chains reach platforms a single player cannot.
steps.teamTip: Put the strongest anchor at the back and the player making the next grab at the front.

steps.title: Generate Swinging Momentum
steps.instruction: Rotate the team in one direction, then reverse at the right moment to grow the swing. Use several controlled movements instead of one huge jump.
steps.teamTip: Have one player count the rhythm so everyone knows when to pull, extend, and release.

steps.title: Release and Catch Safely
steps.instruction: The moving player should extend a free hand toward the target before the throw. Release only the connecting hand, then grip the destination as soon as it overlaps.
steps.teamTip: Keep the remaining chain stable until the thrown player confirms the new grip is secure.

steps.title: Move the Team Forward
steps.instruction: Once the front player lands, they become the new anchor. Transfer teammates one at a time or swing the rest of the chain toward the new position.
steps.teamTip: Slow transfers are usually safer than launching the whole group across a hard gap at once.

steps.title: Recover from Failed Jumps
steps.instruction: When a player misses, everyone still attached should stop swinging and preserve the anchor. Offer a free hand as a rescue point, or reset the short attempt if the chain falls.
steps.teamTip: After repeated failures, change the chain order or use unlocked support items such as extended arms.

quickTips: Always keep at least one player anchored before the team swings.
Releasing near the fastest outward point of a swing converts rotation into distance.
Complete the main route first, then replay for timers, silhouettes, mysterious hands, and photos.
Use the cat drone assistance that appears after a long struggle to bypass stalled sections.

heaveHo2ControlsSwinging.title: Heave Ho 2 Controls and Swinging Techniques
heaveHo2ControlsSwinging.intro: Heave Ho 2 has no traditional jump. Movement comes from rotating your arms and body, gripping with the correct hand, releasing at the right moment, and transferring momentum between surfaces and teammates.

controls.effect: Rotates and directs the character's arms and body around the current grip point.
controls.technique: Use small circular movements near narrow platforms and larger sweeps when building momentum.

controls.input: Hold the left grip button or trigger
controls.effect: Closes the left hand around a nearby surface, object, or teammate.
controls.technique: Keep the left hand attached as an anchor while searching for the next grip with the right.

controls.input: Hold the right grip button or trigger
controls.effect: Closes the right hand around a nearby surface, object, or teammate.
controls.technique: Check the on-screen hand indicator before releasing during crowded human-chain sections.

controls.effect: Opens that hand while the other stays attached if its button is still held.
controls.technique: Release near the fastest outward point of a swing to convert rotation into distance.

controls.input: Alternate left and right grips while rotating upward
controls.effect: Moves the character hand-over-hand along walls, poles, and ledges.
controls.technique: Place the free hand above the anchored hand before releasing the lower grip.

controls.input: Grip the teammate, build momentum, then release the connecting hand
controls.effect: Launches the held player toward a distant surface or teammate.
controls.technique: The thrown player should keep one hand free and point it at the landing target before release.

controls.input: Move an open hand into the teammate and hold the matching grip
controls.effect: Stops a teammate's fall or connects them to the human chain.
controls.technique: Open the catching hand early and keep your body stable instead of chasing the fall.

controls.input: All connected players move in the same circular direction
controls.effect: Builds shared momentum for moving several players across a wide gap.
controls.technique: Choose one caller to set the rhythm and keep the rear anchor attached until the front lands.

heaveHo2MultiplayerCoop.title: Heave Ho 2 Multiplayer and Co-op Options
heaveHo2MultiplayerCoop.intro: Heave Ho 2 brings the series online while keeping shared-screen couch play. Cooperate through the eight worlds or compete in Showdown challenges built around racing, survival, and grabbing objectives.

modes.requirements: Internet; Nintendo versions need Nintendo Switch Online
modes.details: Play campaign levels with remote friends and move every player and object to the goal together.
All players cooperate on the same system with a shared camera and display.

modes.requirements: Internet; Nintendo versions need Nintendo Switch Online
modes.details: Compete in rotating races, survival rounds, and handle-grab contests; first to five wins takes the match.
Play the competitive Showdown playlist locally on a shared screen.

modes.requirements: Players stay within the same gameplay view
modes.details: Local multiplayer uses one shared camera rather than separate per-player views.

modes.requirements: Host owns the Steam version; guests connect via Remote Play
modes.details: Streams the host's local session to remote players for couch-style modes online.

modes.requirements: A Nintendo Switch 2 starts the GameShare session
modes.details: The owner shares Heave Ho 2 with nearby players so they can join without buying a copy.

modes.requirements: GameChat, internet, Nintendo Accounts, and Nintendo Switch Online
modes.details: The owner starts GameShare over GameChat to share the game with remote friends.

heaveHo2WalkthroughWorlds.title: All Eight Heave Ho 2 Worlds
heaveHo2WalkthroughWorlds.intro: Follow the full co-op campaign across eight themed adventures and about 60 levels. Each world adds new traversal rules, team objectives, hazards, and optional challenges.

worlds.name: Flying High
worlds.objective: Move the group across elevated routes and reach the finish around moving transport and exposed gaps.
worlds.tip: Use short, controlled swings around moving platforms before attempting timer or collectible objectives.

worlds.name: Manor Mysteries
worlds.objective: Search the manor, recover the keys each stage needs, and bring the team to the unlocked exit.
worlds.tip: Protect the key carrier with a stable chain and pass the key across gaps in short handoffs.

worlds.name: Spacebound Squad
worlds.objective: Navigate weightless stages and cooperate closely when a level asks the group to fly a spaceship.
worlds.tip: Assign clear roles before vehicle sections so one player positions while others stabilize or prepare the next grab.

worlds.name: Chef's Orders
worlds.objective: Carry and stack the required ingredients on a plate while keeping bulky objects under control.
worlds.tip: Move one ingredient at a time, keep one player anchored, and use deliberate handoffs over throws.

worlds.name: Shipwrecked
worlds.objective: Clear the Shipwrecked adventure by getting every player through each cooperative level.
worlds.tip: Secure the standard clear first, then replay for timer, collectible, and positioning challenges.

worlds.name: Transfolab
worlds.objective: Work through the Transfolab adventure while adapting to each stage's devices and puzzle rules.
worlds.tip: Let one player test each mechanism while the rest keep safe anchor points and a recovery chain.

worlds.name: Cascade Show
worlds.objective: Complete the Cascade Show adventure by coordinating movement across demanding stages.
worlds.tip: Use one caller for release timing and split conflicting optional objectives across separate replays.

worlds.name: Medieval Travels
mechanics: Transporting a king on a throne
worlds.objective: Carry the king through throne-transport levels without tipping him, then finish the campaign.
worlds.tip: Place players on opposite sides, use small synchronized moves, and avoid big swings while the throne is unsupported.

heaveHo2ItemsUnlocks.title: Heave Ho 2 Items, Unlocks and Cosmetics
heaveHo2ItemsUnlocks.intro: Heave Ho 2 mixes world-specific stage objects with equippable support tools. Optional objectives also advance reward tracks containing gifts, cosmetics, crossover skins, and gameplay items.

items.name: Pop Guns
items.effect: A shooting-based tool used in push, pull, shoot, and squeeze challenges.
items.bestUse: Coordinate each shot with the team so the target player or object is ready for the result.

items.name: Drones
items.effect: Part of the interactive object set; a cat drone can appear after a long struggle to help the team forward.
items.bestUse: Use drone assistance to bypass a stalled section, then replay later for optional goals.

items.name: Spaceships
items.effect: A cooperative vehicle used in zero-gravity stages, piloted and stabilized by the group.
items.bestUse: Assign piloting and stabilizing roles before launch instead of everyone pulling a different way.

items.name: Keys
items.effect: Hidden keys must be found and carried to unlock stage exits.
items.bestUse: Protect the key carrier with a stable chain and pass the key across gaps in short steps.

items.name: Ski Lifts and Gondolas
items.effect: Moving transport platforms carry players through icy mountain routes and create timed grabs.
items.bestUse: Keep one player safely attached until the next support is within reach.

items.name: Camera
items.effect: Lets the team capture required photos of specific actions or poses for Photo challenges.
items.bestUse: Equip it for photo objectives and stage the required scene before the final route.

items.name: Ima Rock
items.effect: Freezes the user in midair so another player can use them as a temporary platform.
items.bestUse: Activate it over a gap to create a stable midpoint for a teammate's next grab.

items.name: Arm Spray
items.effect: Extends an ally's arms, making distant platforms and teammates easier to grab.
items.bestUse: Apply it before a long reach, a difficult rescue, or a route where one player must stay anchored far away.

items.name: Umbrella
items.effect: Allows floating and gives the user more control while crossing open space.
items.bestUse: Use it to slow a fall, extend a jump, or approach a distant grab with less downward speed.

items.name: Gifts and Crossover Cosmetics
items.effect: Adds character cosmetics and crossover skins, including an Among Us Crewmate and Henry from Kingdom Come: Deliverance II.
items.bestUse: Replay stages with unfinished objectives, open every gift for the Gift Addict achievement, and finish the reward tracks.

heaveHo2Achievements.title: Heave Ho 2 Achievement Checklist
heaveHo2Achievements.intro: Track all 21 Steam achievements, from finishing the eight campaign worlds to clearing every optional challenge set and reaching 100 percent completion.

heaveHo2SystemRequirements.title: Heave Ho 2 PC Requirements and 1080p Settings
heaveHo2SystemRequirements.intro: The official PC targets cover 1080p at 60 FPS on both Low and High quality. Heave Ho 2 requires a 64-bit version of Windows and 4 GB of available storage.

specs.minimum: Intel Core i5-4670K or AMD FX-8350
specs.recommended: Intel Core i7-6950X or AMD Ryzen 7 2700

specs.minimum: GeForce GTX 760, Radeon R9 270X, or Intel UHD Graphics 620
specs.recommended: GeForce GTX 1080, Radeon RX Vega 56, or Intel Arc A580

settingsCards.name: Minimum-Spec PCs
tips: Start from the official Low Quality target.
Close heavy browser tabs and recording software before hosting online play.
If frames still drop, lower the resolution below 1080p before changing anything else.

settingsCards.name: Recommended-Spec PCs
tips: Use High Quality at 1080p for the official recommended target.
Keep graphics drivers current and ensure the game uses the dedicated GPU on laptops.
Prioritize a stable 60 FPS in co-op so grabs and releases are easier to time.

settingsCards.name: Below-Minimum Hardware
tips: Choose the Low preset and lower resolution in small steps.
Run the game while the laptop is plugged in on its performance power mode.
Avoid simultaneous streaming or recording on older integrated graphics.

faq.title: Heave Ho 2
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to the most common Heave Ho 2 questions before you play.

questions.question: What is Heave Ho 2?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 is a chaotic physics-based co-op party game for 2-4 players. You grab surfaces, form human chains, swing across gaps, and launch teammates toward the goal across eight themed worlds.

questions.question: When did Heave Ho 2 release?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 was released on July 16, 2026 for Windows PC, Nintendo Switch, and Nintendo Switch 2.

questions.question: What platforms is Heave Ho 2 on?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 is available on Steam for Windows PC and on the Nintendo eShop for both Nintendo Switch and Nintendo Switch 2. The Switch 2 version adds local and online GameShare.

questions.question: How many players does Heave Ho 2 support?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 supports 2-4 players in online co-op, online Showdown, shared-screen co-op, and shared-screen Showdown. You can also team up through Steam Remote Play Together.

questions.question: How much does Heave Ho 2 cost?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 costs $9.99 on Steam and the Nintendo eShop. A free demo with local and online co-op is also available.

questions.question: How many levels and worlds are in Heave Ho 2?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 includes eight themed worlds with about 72 co-op levels and 56 randomized versus levels. Each level also offers optional objectives such as timers, silhouettes, and photos.

questions.question: Does Heave Ho 2 have crossplay and GameShare?
questions.answer: Nintendo Switch 2 owners can use local GameShare and online GameShare through GameChat to share Heave Ho 2 with up to three other players. Steam players can use Remote Play Together to bring friends into couch-style modes.

questions.question: Can I play Heave Ho 2 solo?
questions.answer: Heave Ho 2 is built around 2-4 player co-op, but you can start a session alone to explore mechanics. The campaign is designed for teams, so the best experience comes from playing with friends locally or online.
